title: PassbackAI interactive components — an open palette, one primitive per unclear point
description: JSON Schema for the interactive components PassbackAI renders inside a pasted Markdown document. The palette is OPEN and growing; pick the primitive that fits each individual ambiguity: ```single-choice (choose one), ```multi-choice (choose many), ```open-question (write), ```prioritize (order), ```questionnaire (a GROUP of tightly-related questions presented as one unit). Prose is itself a response channel — the reviewer annotates it — so settled thinking stays prose. Each component is a bare JSON object inside its own fenced block; no other fence tag renders (```quiz, ```poll, ```multiple-choice are NOT block types — they show as plain code). A payload validates against exactly one component. Human spec: https://passbackai.com/ask ; raw Markdown: https://passbackai.com/ask.md — this file is kept in sync with them in the same commit.
$comment: Spec revision r7 (2026-07-02). The fence contract: only the component tags named in oneOf render; everything else is a plain code block. This JSON Schema validates the JSON INSIDE a fence — it cannot enforce the fence tag itself, so the renderer contract is documented in /ask.md §2. Wire `version` is the string "1" for every component; `skill_version` is unrelated authoring-build metadata. Guards not expressible here, noted in `description` fields: (a) questionnaire question ids should be unique — duplicates collide answer state but are not rejected; (b) prioritize item ids MUST be unique — a duplicate rejects the block; (c) `recommended` and `routing.return_prompt` cross-field rules. Validate structure here, then consult /ask.md for the full guard list and the fence contract.

routing.description: Include only when the document is sent to someone else. `return_prompt` should contain the literal value of `from` so the renderer can bold the name.

open_field.description: Overrides the default "Other:" label with a directed escape-hatch (e.g. "I need to check with:").

single-choice.title: Single choice (verb: choose one)
single-choice.description: ONE decision, pick exactly one option. The per-ambiguity form of a choice — reach for `questionnaire` only to group several tightly-related questions.

version.description: Always the string "1" — the number 1 is rejected.

id.description: Optional stable id; defaults to the block's document position.

question.description: The prompt. The key must be `question` — never `q`/`text`/`prompt`.

options.description: 2–4 short, genuinely distinct labels (plain strings). Do not include an "Other" entry — the renderer adds a free-text row automatically. Zero real options → use open-question instead.

recommended.description: Mark-only nudge: must exactly match an option label; never pre-selects.

examples.question: Which auth method ships in v1?

multi-choice.title: Multi choice (verb: choose many)
multi-choice.description: ONE decision where more than one option may genuinely apply (features, regions, stakeholders). Never for mutually-exclusive picks — that is single-choice.

version.description: Always the string "1" — the number 1 is rejected.

id.description: Optional stable id; defaults to the block's document position.

question.description: The prompt. The key must be `question` — never `q`/`text`/`prompt`.

options.description: 2–6 short, parallel labels (plain strings). Do not include an "Other" entry — the renderer adds a free-text row automatically.

recommended.description: Mark-only nudge: option labels to badge; each must exactly match an option label; never pre-selects.

open-question.title: Open question (verb: write)
open-question.description: A genuinely open point with no enumerable options — the reviewer types a free-text answer (a name, a date, a description, a reason).

version.description: Always the string "1" — the number 1 is rejected.

id.description: Optional stable id; defaults to the block's document position.

question.description: The prompt. The key must be `question` — never `q`/`text`/`prompt`.

placeholder.description: Optional example text shown inside the empty answer field.

examples.question: What is the data-retention constraint from legal?

questionnaire.title: Questionnaire (verb: group)
questionnaire.description: A GROUP of tightly-related questions presented as one unit; each is a single/multi-select or a pure free-text field. Prefer the standalone per-ambiguity primitives (single-choice / multi-choice / open-question) woven into prose; group only when 3+ questions truly form one decision cluster. Embedded as a ```questionnaire fence, or pasted as a bare JSON object on its own.

version.description: The wire schema version. Always the string "1" — the number 1 is rejected.

skill_version.description: Authoring-skill build tag. Optional metadata; does not gate rendering.

questions.question: Which auth method ships in v1?

id.description: Should be unique within the questionnaire; duplicates are not rejected but collide answer state.

question.description: The prompt. The key must be `question` — never `q`/`text`/`prompt`.

options.description: Plain strings only — an option object is rejected. Do not include an "Other" entry; the renderer adds a free-text row automatically. May be [] for a pure free-text question, which then requires `open_field`.

multi.description: true = checkboxes (multi-select). Must be a boolean — "true"/1 are rejected.

recommended.description: Mark-only nudge: an option label (string), or an array of labels when `multi` is true. Must exactly match an existing option label; a non-matching value is silently ignored. Never pre-selects and never echoed into the answers.

allow_note.description: Always omit — every option-bearing question always shows a note field.

allOf.$comment: A question with no options is pure free-text and must ship an explicit open_field.

prioritize.title: Prioritize (verb: order)
prioritize.description: A short list the user ranks. Embedded only inside a ```prioritize fence (no bare-JSON shortcut). The array order is the suggested starting order.

items.description: Item ids MUST be unique across the array — a duplicate id rejects the whole block. (JSON Schema cannot express per-property uniqueness; enforce it on your side.)
